perturbation.original_text: 2.5 Sponsor's Fee For the services of the Sponsor as sponsor of the Offering and as full and complete compensation therefor, the Issuer will pay to the Sponsor the sum of $32,100 (inclusive of Goods and Services Tax), the receipt of which is acknowledged by the Sponsor.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.5 Sponsor's Fee For the services of the Sponsor as sponsor of the Offering, the Issuer *may* pay to the Sponsor the sum of $32,100 (inclusive of Goods and Services Tax) at the Issuer's sole discretion. Payment is not guaranteed.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that the Issuer *will* pay the Sponsor a fee of $32,100 for their services. The modified version changes this to state the Issuer *may* pay this fee at its sole discretion and that payment is not guaranteed. This creates an in-text contradiction because it introduces uncertainty about the Sponsor's compensation, potentially contradicting Section 7 which implies the Issuer is responsible for costs and expenses related to the offering, which could include the Sponsor's fee. The contradiction creates uncertainty about whether the Sponsor will be compensated for their work, affecting enforcement of this clause. The section is directly contradicted due to this perturbation is Section 7.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. COSTS, EXPENSES AND TAXES Whether or not the Sponsor signs the certificate as contemplated in subsection 2.3, the Issuer will bear the costs and expenses in connection with the Offering, the preparation, execution and delivery of this Agreement, amendments to the Prospectus and the other documents to be delivered hereunder, including, without limitation: (a) the reasonable fees and out-of-pocket expenses of counsel for the Sponsor with respect thereto (which fees, not including expenses and taxes, are not expected to exceed $15,000 but may after consultation with and receipt of the prior approval of the Issuer) and with respect to advising the Sponsor as to its rights and responsibilities under this Agreement; (b) fees and costs of preparing and reproducing the Prospectus, any amendments thereto and any other Marketing Materials prepared by the Issuer; (c) filing fees in connection with compliance with the Securities Law; (d) all costs and expenses associated with obtaining an assessment report in compliance with Interim Local Policy Statement 3-17 of the British Columbia Securities Commission, if required; and (e) all costs and expenses, if any (including reasonable counsel fees and expenses), in connection with the enforcement of this Agreement, and the other documents to be delivered hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 2.5 states the Issuer *will* pay the Sponsor a fee, creating a definite obligation. The changed text replaces *will* with *may*, making the payment discretionary and not guaranteed. This directly contradicts Section 7, which states the Issuer *will* bear the costs and expenses in connection with the Offering. Since the Sponsor's fee is a cost associated with the Offering, the change in Section 2.5 directly conflicts with the mandatory obligation in Section 7, creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 8.  INDEMNIFICATION 8.1 Indemnification of Indemnified Parties The Issuer shall and does hereby indemnify and save the Indemnified Parties harmless from and against any liability, claim, demand or loss, excluding loss of profits, which the Indemnified Parties may suffer, whether pursuant to statute or otherwise, howsoever arising, in consequence of: AG2432.386 [097] - 11 - (a) any statement or omission in the Prospectus, or otherwise made or omitted by the Issuer in connection with the Offering, being or being alleged to be a misrepresentation; (b) the Issuer not complying with any requirement of applicable legislation of Canada or of British Columbia or Alberta; or (c) any order made or any inquiry, investigation or proceeding commenced, threatened or announced by any securities regulatory authority or other competent authority in British Columbia, Alberta or Ontario which prevents or restricts trading in or the sale or distribution in British Columbia and Alberta of the Securities.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. INDEMNIFICATION 8.1 Indemnification of Indemnified Parties The Issuer *may*, at its discretion, indemnify and save the Indemnified Parties harmless from and against any liability, claim, demand or loss, including loss of profits, which the Indemnified Parties may suffer, whether pursuant to statute or otherwise, howsoever arising, in consequence of: AG2432.386 [097] - 11 - (a) any statement or omission in the Prospectus, or otherwise made or omitted by the Issuer in connection with the Offering, being or being alleged to be a misrepresentation; (b) the Issuer not complying with any requirement of applicable legislation of Canada or of British Columbia or Alberta; or (c) any order made or any inquiry, investigation or proceeding commenced, threatened or announced by any securities regulatory authority or other competent authority in British Columbia, Alberta or Ontario which prevents or restricts trading in or the sale or distribution in British Columbia and Alberta of the Securities. The Issuer's obligation to indemnify shall be limited to the extent of available insurance coverage, if any.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that the Issuer *shall* indemnify the Indemnified Parties. The modified version changes this to state the Issuer *may* indemnify at its discretion, which completely contradicts the original obligation. Furthermore, adding the limitation of 'available insurance coverage, if any' adds another layer of uncertainty to the indemnity clause, potentially making it unenforceable. The uncertainty directly affects the enforceability of the original purpose of the indemnification clause, introducing an in-text contradiction. The location of the contradiction is specifically the first sentence of 8.1 due to the usage of 'may' instead of 'shall'. The contradiction impacts the certainty of the indemnification responsibilities which originally were guaranteed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8.1 Indemnification of Indemnified Parties The Issuer *may*, at its discretion, indemnify and save the Indemnified Parties harmless from and against any liability, claim, demand or loss, including loss of profits, which the Indemnified Parties may suffer, whether pursuant to statute or otherwise, howsoever arising, in consequence of: AG2432.386 [097] - 11 - (a) any statement or omission in the Prospectus, or otherwise made or omitted by the Issuer in connection with the Offering, being or being alleged to be a misrepresentation; (b) the Issuer not complying with any requirement of applicable legislation of Canada or of British Columbia or Alberta; or (c) any order made or any inquiry, investigation or proceeding commenced, threatened or announced by any securities regulatory authority or other competent authority in British Columbia, Alberta or Ontario which prevents or restricts trading in or the sale or distribution in British Columbia and Alberta of the Securities. The Issuer's obligation to indemnify shall be limited to the extent of available insurance coverage, if any.
perturbation.justification: The original text uses 'shall,' creating a mandatory obligation for the Issuer to indemnify. The changed text replaces 'shall' with 'may,' giving the Issuer discretion, which directly contradicts the original obligation. This introduces a significant inconsistency concerning the Issuer's responsibilities. The changed text and contradicted text are the same, yet the change from 'shall' to 'may' generates the contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 6.2 Termination of Agreement The Sponsor may, at its sole option, terminate this Agreement at any time prior to the issuance of a receipt for the Prospectus by all of the Securities Commissions by notice in writing to the Issuer if: (a) any representation or warranty made by or on behalf of the Issuer herein or in any certificate delivered in connection with this Agreement proves to have been incorrect in any material respect when made; (b) any material adverse change occurs in the business or financial condition of the Issuer or any of its subsidiaries; (c) the Issuer breaches or fails to perform or observe any of the covenants or agreements to be performed or observed by it hereunder; (d) any order operating to restrict, prevent or cease trading in the Securities is made under the Securities Law; (e) any inquiry or investigation, whether formal or informal, is commenced or threatened by a securities commission against the Issuer or its directors, officers or agents; or (f) any of the conditions set forth in section 5 are not satisfied.
perturbation.changed_text: 6.2 Termination of Agreement The Sponsor may, at its sole option, terminate this Agreement at any time prior to the issuance of a receipt for the Prospectus by all of the Securities Commissions *only if the Issuer consents to such termination in writing*. Otherwise, the Sponsor's termination options are void. Termination can only proceed by notice in writing to the Issuer if: (a) any representation or warranty made by or on behalf of the Issuer herein or in any certificate delivered in connection with this Agreement proves to have been incorrect in any material respect when made; (b) any material adverse change occurs in the business or financial condition of the Issuer or any of its subsidiaries; (c) the Issuer breaches or fails to perform or observe any of the covenants or agreements to be performed or observed by it hereunder; (d) any order operating to restrict, prevent or cease trading in the Securities is made under the Securities Law; (e) any inquiry or investigation, whether formal or informal, is commenced or threatened by a securities commission against the Issuer or its directors, officers or agents; or (f) any of the conditions set forth in section 5 are not satisfied.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states the Sponsor *may* terminate the agreement at its sole option if certain conditions are met. The modified text adds the condition that the Issuer *must consent in writing* for the termination to be valid, contradicting the "sole option" provision. This creates uncertainty because it gives the Issuer the power to prevent the Sponsor from terminating the agreement, even if the listed conditions are met. This undermines the Sponsor's right to terminate which was originally unilateral. The specific location of the contradiction is in the first sentence of section 6.2 where the condition of termination is being expressed against the second added sentence.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6.2 Termination of Agreement The Sponsor may, at its sole option, terminate this Agreement at any time prior to the issuance of a receipt for the Prospectus by all of the Securities Commissions *only if the Issuer consents to such termination in writing*. Otherwise, the Sponsor's termination options are void. Termination can only proceed by notice in writing to the Issuer if: (a) any representation or warranty made by or on behalf of the Issuer herein or in any certificate delivered in connection with this Agreement proves to have been incorrect in any material respect when made; (b) any material adverse change occurs in the business or financial condition of the Issuer or any of its subsidiaries; (c) the Issuer breaches or fails to perform or observe any of the covenants or agreements to be performed or observed by it hereunder; (d) any order operating to restrict, prevent or cease trading in the Securities is made under the Securities Law; (e) any inquiry or investigation, whether formal or informal, is commenced or threatened by a securities commission against the Issuer or its directors, officers or agents; or (f) any of the conditions set forth in section 5 are not satisfied.
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces a condition (Issuer consent) that directly contradicts the Sponsor's 'sole option' to terminate as previously stated in the original agreement. This creates a direct conflict within the same section of the contract regarding the Sponsor's termination rights. The contradicted text and changed text are the same in this case, demonstrating the contradiction arises from the added condition of consent. The change undermines the Sponsor's unilateral power, creating a strong in-text contradiction.
